headline: Did Policy Uncertainty and 2026 Sales Signals Just Shift First Solar's (FSLR) Investment Narrative?
summary: In early March 2026, analysts adjusted their views on First Solar after the company signalled that 2026 net sales could fall short of earlier expectations amid U.S. policy uncertainty and permitting delays, even as it moves to open a new South Carolina finishing line to improve logistics, tariffs exposure, and local sourcing. At the same time, some investors highlighted First Solar’s thin-film Cadmium Telluride technology and strong demand for U.S.-made solar modules supported by...

headline: First Solar Expansion And Institutional Support Shape Valuation And Risk Profile
summary: First Solar (NasdaqGS:FSLR) plans to open a new module finishing line in South Carolina in late 2026 to expand its U.S. manufacturing footprint. The facility is intended to support logistics, reduce exposure to tariff risks, and increase reliance on U.S. based inputs. Institutional investors, including Polen Capital, have cited First Solar as a key contributor to portfolio performance and pointed to its differentiated technology and U.S. policy support. First Solar focuses on thin film...

headline: 3 Profitable Stocks to Keep an Eye On
summary: Profitability is a key measure of business strength. Companies with high margins have proven they can generate consistent earnings while maintaining financial discipline.

headline: GLJ Research Lowers First Solar, Inc. (FSLR) from Buy to Hold
summary: First Solar, Inc. (NASDAQ:FSLR) is one of the Goldman Sachs Solar and Green Energy Stocks: Top 10 Stock Picks. On March 4, 2026, GLJ Research lowered First Solar, Inc. (NASDAQ:FSLR) to Hold from Buy. On February 25, 2026, Reuters reported that First Solar, Inc. (NASDAQ:FSLR) anticipated net sales of $4.9 billion to $5.2 billion in […]
